company.name: EchoMetrix, Inc.
company.business_description: EchoMetrix develops and sells Sentry Parental Controls, a software program that monitors and records activity on target computers including web history, chat conversations, and instant message conversations. The company also operates The Pulse, a web-based market research software that aggregates and analyzes consumer opinion and sentiment from user-generated digital social media content.

case_info.legal_authority: Section 5(a) of the Federal Trade Commission Act, 15 U.S.C. § 45(a)

complaint.factual_background: EchoMetrix, Inc. sold Sentry Parental Controls software to parents to monitor their children's online activity, including instant message conversations, websites visited, and applications used. In June 2009, EchoMetrix launched a separate web-based market research product called The Pulse, which aggregated consumer sentiment from online conversations. Without adequately disclosing this to Sentry purchasers, EchoMetrix fed data collected from children's online activity through the Sentry software into the Pulse database, which was then sold to third-party marketers and advertisers. The only potential notice given to Sentry purchasers was a vague statement buried approximately 30 paragraphs deep in a small scroll box EULA, which referred generically to 'anonymous reporting for internal and external clients' without identifying Pulse or the sharing of children's data with marketers.

representations_made.description: EchoMetrix represented that Sentry software would track all online activity on a computer, including instant message conversations, websites visited, and applications used by children — without disclosing that this collected data would be shared with third-party marketers through Pulse
representations_made.quoted_text: 16. In numerous instances in connection with the advertising, marketing, promotion, offering for sale, or sale of the Sentry Parental Controls software, EchoMetrix has represented, directly or indirectly, expressly or by implication, that the software would track all online activity on aa computer, including instant message conversations, websites visited, and applications used by children using the computer.

counts.title: Deceptive Omission — Failure to Disclose Data Sharing with Third-Party Marketers
counts.representation: EchoMetrix represented, directly or indirectly, expressly or by implication, that Sentry software would track all online activity on a computer, including instant message conversations, websites visited, and applications used by children using the computer
counts.fact: EchoMetrix failed to adequately disclose that information revealed by children in connection with online activity monitored by Sentry would be disclosed to third-party marketers through Pulse; this material information was not adequately communicated to consumers and would have been material to their decision to purchase Sentry
counts.conclusion: The failure to adequately disclose the material information set forth in Paragraph 17, in light of the representation described in Paragraph 16, constitutes a deceptive act or practice in violation of Section 5(a) of the FTC Act, 15 U.S.C. § 45(a).

definitions.Defendant: EchoMetrix, Inc., and its successors and assigns.
definitions.Sentry: the Sentry Parental Controls software program, or any other similar software program marketed or sold by Defendant that can monitor and/or record activity on a computer.
definitions.Collected Information: any information or data collected by and transmitted from Sentry, from a computer on which Sentry is installed to any computer server owned by, operated by, or operated for the benefit of, Defendant.
definitions.Commerce: as defined in Section 4 of the FTC Act, 15 U.S.C. § 44.
definitions.Computer: any desktop or laptop computer, handheld device, telephone, or other electronic product or device that has a platform on which to download, install, or run any software program, code, script, or other content.
definitions.Personnel Necessary to Support Sentry: only those persons, whether employed by EchoMetrix or a third party, who require access to Collected Information to maintain the technical functioning of Sentry and who use the Collected Information solely for that purpose.
definitions.Registered Sentry User: a purchaser of Sentry, who is authorized (via login and password) to access Collected Information from a specific account or accounts.
definitions.Pulse: The Pulse service, or any substantially similar service marketed by Defendant that aggregates, collects, measures, or analyzes data from user-generated digital content (including but not limited to Internet forums, message boards, chats, blogs, and instant message conversations) for use by third parties.

provisions.title: Ban on Use of Information from Sentry
provisions.summary: Defendant is permanently enjoined from disclosing Collected Information to anyone other than Personnel Necessary to Support Sentry or a Registered Sentry User for the sole purpose of accessing that user's account.

requirements.description: Defendant and all persons in active concert with it are permanently enjoined and restrained from disclosing Collected Information to anyone other than Personnel Necessary to Support Sentry or a Registered Sentry User for the sole purpose of accessing that user's account.
requirements.quoted_text: IT IS HEREBY ORDERED that Defendant, operating directly or through any corporation, partnership, subsidiary, division, trade name, or other device, and its officers, agents, servants, employees and attorneys, and all other persons who are in active concert or participation with them who receive actual notice of this Order, by personal service or otherwise, are hereby permanently enjoined and restrained from disclosing Collected Information to anyone other than Personnel Necessary to Support Sentry or aa Registered Sentry User for the sole purpose of accessing that user's account.

provisions.title: Destruction of Collected Information
provisions.summary: Defendant must destroy, within 5 days of entry of the Order, any Collected Information transferred to or available in any database or server used by Pulse, except for information only available to Registered Sentry Users.

requirements.description: Defendant shall, no later than five (5) days after the date of entry of this Order, destroy any Collected Information that was transferred to or is available in any database or server used by Pulse, provided that Collected Information on a database or server only available to Registered Sentry Users need not be destroyed.
requirements.quoted_text: IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that Defendant, operating directly or through any corporation, partnership, subsidiary, division, trade name, or other device, shall, no later than five (5) days after the date of entry of this Order, destroy any Collected Information that was transferred to or is available in any database or server used by Pulse, provided, however, that Collected Information on aa database or server that is only available to Registered Sentry Users need not be destroyed.

provisions.title: Compliance Reporting
provisions.summary: Defendant must notify the FTC of structural changes for 4 years, submit sworn annual compliance reports starting 60 days after entry, notify of bankruptcy within 15 days, and send all reports by overnight courier or first-class mail with simultaneous electronic copy.

requirements.description: For a period of four (4) years from the date of entry of this Order, Defendant shall notify the Commission of any changes in its structure or any business entity that Defendant directly or indirectly controls, or has an ownership interest in, that may affect compliance obligations, at least thirty (30) days prior to such change (or as soon as practicable if less than 30 days' notice is possible).
requirements.quoted_text: A. For aa period of four (4) years from the date of entry of this Order, Defendant shall notify the Commission of any changes in its structure or any business entity that Defendant directly or indirectly controls, or has an ownership interest in, that may affect compliance obligations arising under this Order, including but not limited to: incorporation or other organization; aa dissolution, assignment, sale, merger, or other action; the creation or dissolution of aa subsidiary, parent, or affiliate that engages in any acts or practices subject to this Order; or aa change in the business name or address, at least thirty (30) days prior to such change, provided that, with respect to any such change in the business entity about which Defendant learns less than thirty (30) days prior to the date such action is to take place, Defendant shall notify the Commission as soon as is practicable after obtaining such knowledge.

requirements.description: Sixty (60) days after the date of entry of this Order, and annually thereafter for a period of four (4) years, Defendant shall provide a written report to the Commission, sworn under penalty of perjury, setting forth in detail the manner and form in which they have complied and are complying with this Order, including a copy of each acknowledgment of receipt of this Order and any other changes required to be reported under Subsection A.
requirements.quoted_text: B. Sixty (60) days after the date of entry of this Order, and annually thereafter for aa period of four (4) years, Defendant shall provide aa written report to the Commission, which is true and accurate and sworn to under penalty of perjury, setting forth in detail the manner and form in which they have complied and are complying with this Order. This report shall include, but not be limited to: 1. AA copy of each acknowledgment of receipt of this Order, obtained pursuant to the Section titled "Distribution of Order;" and 2. Any other changes required to be reported under Subsection AA of this Section.

requirements.description: Defendant shall notify the Commission of the filing of a bankruptcy petition by such Defendant within fifteen (15) days of filing.
requirements.quoted_text: C. Defendant shall notify the Commission of the filing of aa bankruptcy petition by such Defendant within fifteen (15) days of filing.

requirements.description: Defendant shall send all reports and notifications required by this Order by overnight courier (not the U.S. Postal Service) to the Associate Director for Enforcement, FTC, or alternatively by first-class mail with a simultaneous electronic version sent to DEBrief@ftc.gov.
requirements.quoted_text: D. For purposes ofthis Order, Defendant shall, unless otherwise directed by the Commission's authorized representatives, send by overnight courier (not the u.S. Postal Service) -5- Case 2:10-cv-05516-DRH -ARL Document 1-2 Filed 11/30/10 Page 6 of 10 all reports and notifications to the Commission that are required by this Order to: Associate Director for Enforcement Federal Trade Commission 600 Pennsylvania Avenue, N.W. Washington, D.C. 20580 Attn: FTC v. EchoMetrix, Inc. Provided that, in lieu of an overnight courier, Defendant may send such reports or notifications by first-class mail, but only if Defendant contemporaneously sends an electronic version of such report or notification to the Commission at: DEBrief@ftc.gov.

requirements.description: For purposes of compliance reporting and monitoring, the Commission is authorized to communicate directly with Defendant.
requirements.quoted_text: E. For purposes of the compliance reporting and monitoring required by this Order, the Commission is authorized to communicate directly with Defendant.

provisions.title: Compliance Monitoring
provisions.summary: The FTC is authorized to monitor and investigate Defendant's compliance, including requiring written reports, document production, depositions, and facility access, as well as using discovery procedures and posing as consumers or suppliers.

requirements.description: Within ten (10) days of receipt of written notice from a Commission representative, Defendant shall submit additional written reports sworn under penalty of perjury, produce documents for inspection and copying, appear for deposition, and provide entry during normal business hours to any business location in Defendant's possession or control.
requirements.quoted_text: A. Within ten (10) days of receipt of written notice from aa representative of the Commission, Defendant shall submit additional written reports, which are true and accurate and sworn to under penalty of peIjury; produce documents for inspection and copying; appear for deposition; and provide entry during normal business hours to any business location in Defendant's possession or direct or indirect control to inspect the business operation;

requirements.description: The Commission is authorized to use all other lawful means, including obtaining discovery from any person using the procedures described in Fed. R. Civ. P. 30, 31, 33, 34, 36, 45, and 69.
requirements.quoted_text: 1. Obtaining discovery from any person, without further leave of court, using the procedures described in Fed. R. Civ. P. 30, 31, 33, 34, 36, 45, and 69; and

requirements.description: The Commission is authorized to have its representatives pose as consumers and suppliers to Defendant, its employees, or any other entity managed or controlled in whole or in part by Defendant, without the necessity of identification or prior notice.
requirements.quoted_text: 2. Having its representatives pose as consumers and suppliers to Defendant, its employees, or any other entity managed or controlled in whole or in part by Defendant, without the necessity of identification or prior notice.

requirements.description: Defendant shall permit representatives of the Commission to interview any officer, director, consultant, independent contractor, representative, agent, or employee who has agreed to such an interview, relating in any way to any conduct subject to this Order. The person interviewed may have counsel present.
requirements.quoted_text: C. Defendant shall permit representatives of the Commission to interview any officer, director, consultant, independent contractor, representative, agent, or employee who has agreed to such an interview, relating in any way to any conduct subject to this Order. The person interviewed may have counsel present.

provisions.title: Record-Keeping Requirements
provisions.summary: Defendant must create and retain for seven years specific records related to Sentry and Pulse, including accounting records, personnel records, customer files, complaints, advertising materials, and all documents necessary to demonstrate full compliance with this Order.

requirements.description: For a period of seven (7) years from the date of entry of this Order, Defendant and its agents, employees, officers, corporations, and those in active concert or participation with them must create and retain accounting records reflecting revenues generated by sale of Sentry and Pulse and disbursement of such revenues.
requirements.quoted_text: A. Accounting records that reflect the revenues generated by sale of Sentry and Pulse, and the disbursement of such revenues;

requirements.description: Retain personnel records accurately reflecting the name, address, and telephone number of each person employed in any capacity (including as an independent contractor) who is involved in advertising, marketing, promotion, offering for sale, sale, or distribution of Sentry or Pulse; that person's job title or position; the date commenced work; and the date and reason for termination, if applicable.
requirements.quoted_text: B. Personnel records accurately reflecting: the name, address, and telephone number of each person employed in any capacity by such business, including as an independent contractor, -7- Case 2:10-cv-05516-DRH -ARL Document 1-2 Filed 11/30/10 Page 8 of 10 who is involved in the advertising, marketing, promotion, offering for sale, sale, or distribution of Sentry or Pulse; that person's job title or position; the date upon which the person commenced work; and the date and reason for the person's termination, if applicable;

requirements.description: Retain customer files containing the names, addresses, phone numbers, dollar amounts paid, and description of product purchased, for each purchaser of Sentry and of Pulse, to the extent such information is obtained in the ordinary course of business.
requirements.quoted_text: C. Customer files containing the names, addresses, phone numbers, dollar amounts paid, and description of product purchased, for each purchaser of Sentry and of Pulse, to the extent such information is obtained in the ordinary course of business;

requirements.description: Retain complaints and refund requests (whether received directly or indirectly, such as through a third party), including reports of disclosure of personal information claimed to be associated with the use of Sentry or Pulse, and any responses to those complaints or requests.
requirements.quoted_text: D. Complaints and refund requests (whether received directly or indirectly, such as through aa third party), including, but not limited to, reports of disclosure of personal information claimed to be associated with the use of Sentry or Pulse, and any responses to those complaints or requests;

requirements.description: Retain copies of all advertisements, promotional materials, sales scripts, training materials, websites, end-user license agreements, frequently asked questions, privacy policies, and similar documents relating to Defendant's dissemination of Sentry.
requirements.quoted_text: E. Copies of all advertisements, promotional materials, sales scripts, training materials, websites, end-user license agreements, frequently asked questions, privacy policies, and similar documents relating to Defendant's dissemination of Sentry; and

requirements.description: Retain all records and documents necessary to demonstrate full compliance with each provision of this Order, including copies of acknowledgments of receipt of this Order required by the 'Distribution of Order' and 'Acknowledgment of Receipt of Order' sections, and all reports submitted to the Commission pursuant to the 'Compliance Reporting' section.
requirements.quoted_text: F. All records and documents necessary to demonstrate full compliance with each provision of this Order, including, but not limited to, copies of acknowledgments of receipt of this Order, required by the Section titled "Distribution of Order" and the Section titled "Acknowledgment of Receipt of Order," and all reports submitted to the Commission pursuant to the Section of this Order titled "Compliance Reporting."

provisions.title: Distribution of Order
provisions.summary: Defendant must deliver copies of the Order to principals, officers, directors, managers, relevant employees, agents, and successor business entities within specified timeframes, and must obtain signed acknowledgments of receipt within 30 days of delivery.

requirements.description: For a period of four (4) years from the date of entry of this Order, Defendant shall deliver a copy of this Order to: (1) all of its principals, officers, directors, and managers; (2) all of its employees, agents, and representatives who engage in conduct related to the subject matter of this Order; and (3) any business entity resulting from any change in structure set forth in Subsection A of the Compliance Reporting section. For current personnel, delivery shall be within five (5) days of service of this Order upon Defendant. For new personnel, delivery shall occur prior to their assuming their responsibilities. For any business entity resulting from a change in structure, delivery shall be at least ten (10) days prior to the change in structure.
requirements.quoted_text: A. Defendant shall deliver aa copy ofthis Order to: (1) all of its principals, officers, directors, -8- Case 2:10-cv-05516-DRH -ARL Document 1-2 Filed 11/30/10 Page 9 of 10 and managers; (2) all of its employees, agents, and representatives who engage in conduct related to the subject matter of this Order; and (3) any business entity resulting from any change in structure set forth in Subsection AA of the Section titled "Compliance Reporting." For current personnel, delivery shall be within five (5) days of service of this Order upon Defendant. For new personnel, delivery shall occur prior to their assuming their responsibilities. For any business entity resulting from any change in structure set forth in Subsection AA of the Section titled "Compliance Reporting," delivery shall be at least ten (10) days prior to the change in structure.
requirements.deadline: 5 days for current personnel; prior to assuming responsibilities for new personnel; 10 days prior for successor entities

requirements.description: Defendant must secure a signed and dated statement acknowledging receipt of the Order, within thirty (30) days of delivery, from all persons receiving a copy of the Order pursuant to this section.
requirements.quoted_text: B. Defendant must secure aa signed and dated statement acknowledging receipt of the Order, within thirty (30) days of delivery, from all persons receiving aa copy of the Order pursuant to this Section.

provisions.title: Acknowledgment of Receipt of Order
provisions.summary: Defendant must submit a truthful sworn statement to the FTC acknowledging receipt of this Order within five business days of receiving it.

requirements.description: Defendant, within five (5) business days of receipt of this Order as entered by the Court, shall submit to the Commission a truthful sworn statement acknowledging receipt of this Order.
requirements.quoted_text: IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that Defendant, within five (5) business days of receipt of this Order as entered by the Court, shall submit to the Commission aa truthful sworn statement acknowledging receipt of this Order.

provisions.title: Retention of Jurisdiction
provisions.summary: The Court retains jurisdiction of this matter for purposes of construction, modification, and enforcement of this Order.

requirements.description: This Court shall retain jurisdiction of this matter for purposes of construction, modification, and enforcement of this Order.
requirements.quoted_text: IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that this Court shaH retain jurisdiction of this matter for purposes of construction, modification, and enforcement of this Order.

duration.termination_text: IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that this Court shall retain jurisdiction of this matter for purposes of construction, modification, and enforcement of this Order.

takeaway_brief: EchoMetrix sold parental monitoring software while secretly feeding children's online activity data to a third-party market research product sold to advertisers.
takeaway_full: EchoMetrix, Inc. sold Sentry Parental Controls software to monitor children's online activity but failed to disclose that children's conversations and browsing data collected through Sentry would be fed into its separate Pulse market research platform and sold to third-party marketers and advertisers. The FTC required EchoMetrix to ban all use of information collected through the Sentry software, destroy all previously collected data, and comply with ongoing compliance reporting, monitoring, recordkeeping, and distribution requirements.
